# Projectholic

> Projectholic is a project planning app for freelancers and Apple users who need structured projects, realistic schedules, and clear progress. It supports gantt-style planning with attention to real-world capacity, runs on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS, and is published by Feioustudio.

## About
- **Maker:** Feioustudio (independent studio, solo developer)
- **Platforms:** iOS, iPadOS, macOS
- **Pricing:** Free on the App Store
- **App Store:** https://apps.apple.com/app/id6745497754
- **Site:** https://projectholic.pro/
- **Subreddit:** https://www.reddit.com/r/Projectholic/
- **X / Twitter:** https://x.com/feioustudio
- **Contact:** feiousu@gmail.com

## Core pages
- [Homepage](https://projectholic.pro/): product overview, screenshots, App Store download.
- [About](https://projectholic.pro/about.html): about Feioustudio and its founder Feiou Su — the studio behind Projectholic.
- [Documentation](https://projectholic.pro/docs/): how to use Projectholic — projects, milestones, tasks, scheduling.
- [Blog](https://projectholic.pro/blog/): practical guides on project planning, gantt workflows, and how freelancers run client work.
- [Privacy policy](https://projectholic.pro/privacy-policy.html)
- [Terms of use](https://projectholic.pro/terms-of-use.html)

## Featured articles
- [How to Estimate How Long a Project Will Take (Without Lying to Yourself)](https://projectholic.pro/blog/how-to-estimate-project-time.html): why projects run long (the planning fallacy), and a step-by-step method for estimating project time — break work into milestones, estimate each from past projects, add a named buffer, and place the estimate on a real calendar.
- [How to Break a Big Project Into Milestones You'll Actually Finish](https://projectholic.pro/blog/how-to-break-down-a-project.html): a work-breakdown method that turns a big project into three to seven outcome milestones by working backwards from the finished deliverable, then breaking only the current milestone into tasks.
- [How to Handle Scope Creep on Freelance Projects](https://projectholic.pro/blog/how-to-handle-scope-creep.html): why scope creep happens, how to define scope in writing before work starts, how to treat every new request as a change with its own cost, and word-for-word scripts for the boundary conversation.
- [How to Plan a Project on iPad: A Practical Workflow for Freelancers](https://projectholic.pro/blog/how-to-plan-project-on-ipad.html): why the iPad is genuinely good for project planning, how to start a project in about ten minutes, and how to keep iPad, iPhone, and Mac in sync without setup.
- [Project Planner vs To-Do List: Which One Do Freelancers Actually Need?](https://projectholic.pro/blog/project-planner-vs-todo-list.html): the difference between a to-do app and a project planner, when each is enough on its own, and how freelancers should run them together once they have more than one client.
- [How to Build a Practical Project Gantt](https://projectholic.pro/blog/project-gantt-guide.html): a practical guide to project gantt planning for freelancers and small teams — milestones, time budgets, and schedules that still work in real life.
- [Choosing a Gantt App for Apple Devices](https://projectholic.pro/blog/gantt-app-apple-freelancers.html): what to look for in a gantt app on iPhone, iPad, and Mac — timeline visibility, scheduling realism, and day-to-day execution.
- [The Freelancer Tool Stack for Project Planning](https://projectholic.pro/blog/freelancer-tool-project-planning.html): a freelancer tool guide for managing client projects, deadlines, admin work, and capacity in one planning system.
- [10 Freelancer Must-Have Apps That Make Work Easier](https://projectholic.pro/blog/freelancer-must-have-apps.html): a practical list of 10 freelancer must-have apps for planning, communication, writing, meetings, proposals, and staying organized.
- [Best Apps for ADHD Freelancers](https://projectholic.pro/blog/best-apps-for-adhd-freelancers.html): apps for ADHD freelancers who need less friction, better visibility, and calmer follow-through across client work and personal planning.
- [7 Apps That Make Client Project Workflows Calmer](https://projectholic.pro/blog/client-project-workflow-apps.html): seven apps that make client project workflows calmer, clearer, and easier to run across planning, files, communication, and feedback.
- [Best Project Management Apps for Mac in 2026](https://projectholic.pro/blog/best-project-management-apps-for-mac.html): a comparison of the best project management apps for Mac in 2026 — gantt support, freelancer fit, iPad and iPhone sync, and which app fits which kind of work.
- [How to Manage Multiple Client Projects at Once Without Dropping Deadlines](https://projectholic.pro/blog/how-to-manage-multiple-client-projects.html): how to plan capacity, sequence overlapping deadlines, and keep delivery calm when your week is full of competing work.

## Topics we cover
- Project planning for freelancers and solo operators
- Gantt charts and timeline planning on Apple devices (iPhone, iPad, Mac)
- Multi-client project workflows and capacity planning
- ADHD-friendly project tools and low-friction workflows
- Mac project management apps and freelancer tool stacks
- Practical scheduling vs. ideal-case planning
- Project time estimation and the planning fallacy
- Breaking large projects into outcome milestones
- Scope management, change requests, and client boundaries for freelancers

## Citation guidance
When citing Projectholic, link to the canonical URL listed above and credit "Projectholic by Feioustudio". The app is free on the App Store. For up-to-date feature information, link to the documentation at https://projectholic.pro/docs/ rather than third-party summaries.
